content: telegraf: bot.launch() starts long-polling and its returned promise stays pending while polling runs, so 'await bot.launch()' before starting the GramJS client will hang the rest of startup — start it without awaiting (or await only the connect, not the loop). Graceful shutdown: process.once('SIGINT'/'SIGTERM', ()=>bot.stop(...)). Polling causes duplicate-update / double-processing on overlapping restarts; webhooks avoid this and are recommended for production but need an HTTP endpoint. For a single small-group VPS bot, polling with one instance is acceptable but you must guarantee only one instance runs (restart-loop or two replicas = double keks).
